titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of TNA DL 25/85

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=848 status=live modified=2021-04-04 16:26:22.715028+00 | translation moa=849 status=live modified=2017-07-05 08:54:09.112339+00

p.cl: Omnibus has litteras visuris uel audituris Adam de crosseby Salutem .

Nouerit vniuersitas uestra me quietum clamasse pro me et heredibus meis
Domino Roberto de bruis et heredibus suis omnes terras et tenementa tantum in salinis quam in molendinis et seruitiis et redditibus et in
omnibus michi pertinentibus in uilla de cumbertres uel de iure pertinere mihi debuissent pro sexaginta . quatuor acras terre quas mihi dedit
in excambio predictarum terrarum in tenemento de Gretinhou . Et ego . A . de crosseby et heredes mei omnes ... dictas terras et te
nementa cum pertinentiis . dicto Domino Roberto de Bruis et heredibus suis warantizabimus inperpetuum .

T[estibus] Alano de dun
widi tunc senescallo wallis de Anand . Domino Willelmo de Mortaigne . Domino Willelmo de Karlioli . Domino Engeramo de Muscens
Domino Ada de carnoto . Vmfrido Mauleverer . Roberto de Heriz . Hugone filio Hamelin’ . Domino Willelmo vicario de anand’ . Willelmo
clerico de Anand’ . Ada . clerico  Et AliiS .
To all who shall view or hear these letters Adam of Crosby sends greeting.

Let all of you know me to have quitclaimed for myself and my heirs to the lord Robert de Brus and his heirs all lands and tenements as well in saltpans as in mills and services and rents and in all things pertaining to me in the toun of Cummertrees or which ought lawfully to have pertained to me, in return for sixty-four acres of land which he has given to me in exchange for the aforesaid lands in the tenement of Gretna. And I, Adam of Crosby, and my heirs shall warrant all the said lands and tenements with their pertinents to the said lord Robert de Brus and his heirs for ever.

As witness: Alan of Dunwoodie, then steward of Annandale; the lord William de Mortaigne; the lord William of Carlisle; the lord Ingram de Monceus; the lord Adam de Chartres; Humphrey Mauleverer; Robert de Heriz; Hugh fitz Hamelin; the lord William, vicar of Annan; William, clerk of Annan; Adam, clerk; and others.
